title: The No-Nonsense Guide to How to Sell Ebooks from Kenya — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: As a digital creator in Kenya, you're no stranger to the frustration of getting blocked by PayPal or Stripe. Every time you try to set up a payment system for your ebooks, you're met with a message stating that your account has been restricted due to 'unusual activity' or 'geographic restrictions'. You've tried using local payment gateways, but they come with hefty fees and limited functionality. You're not alone – hundreds of creators just like you have been stuck in this cycle of frustration. It's time to break free from these limitations and start selling your ebooks without the hassle of PayPal or Stripe. In this guide, we'll show you how to do just that – no technical expertise required.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

The reason why traditional payment gateways like PayPal and Stripe don't work for creators in Kenya is because of their strict Know-Your-Customer (KYC) policies. These policies require creators to verify their identity and address before allowing them to receive payments, which is a significant barrier to entry for many creators. Additionally, these gateways also have strict rules against certain types of transactions, such as those involving cryptocurrencies or high-risk countries. This is where blockchain-based payment solutions come in – they offer a more open and inclusive approach to transactions, allowing creators to receive payments without the need for KYC or other restrictions. By using a blockchain-based payment solution, you can break free from the limitations of traditional payment gateways and start selling your ebooks to customers around the world.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you can start selling your ebooks using a blockchain-based payment solution, you'll need a few things. First, you'll need to create a digital product – in this case, an ebook – that you can sell to customers. You'll also need to set up a payment solution, such as the Full Stack Bundle, which is a simple and secure way to accept payments from customers. Additionally, you'll need to have a way to deliver your ebook to customers once a payment has been made. This can be as simple as sending a download link via email or using a digital delivery platform to send your ebook directly to the customer's computer.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

Setting up your payment and delivery pipeline is a straightforward process. First, you'll need to sign up for the Full Stack Bundle, which will provide you with the necessary tools to set up a payment solution and deliver your ebook to customers. Once you've signed up, you'll need to configure your payment settings to accept payments in your preferred cryptocurrency, such as Bitcoin or Ethereum. You'll also need to set up a delivery system to send your ebook to customers once a payment has been made. This can be as simple as sending a download link via email or using a digital delivery platform to send your ebook directly to the customer's computer. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to a range of tools and features that make it easy to set up and manage your payment and delivery pipeline.
The blockchain has no map of restricted countries — a wallet in Lagos processes identically to one in London.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and start selling your ebooks. The first step is to create a sales page for your ebook, where customers can learn more about your product and make a purchase. You can use a platform like WordPress or Wix to create a sales page, or you can use a more specialized platform like Gumroad or Sellfy. Once your sales page is set up, you can start promoting your ebook to customers and accepting payments through your payment solution. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to a range of tools and features that make it easy to track your sales and manage your customer relationships.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

Once you've made your first sale, it's time to start scaling your business. This means increasing your marketing efforts to reach more customers, improving your sales page to increase conversions, and optimizing your payment and delivery pipeline to make it more efficient. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to a range of tools and features that make it easy to scale your business. You'll be able to track your sales and customer relationships, and make data-driven decisions to improve your business. You'll also be able to access a range of marketing tools and resources to help you promote your ebook and reach more customers.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most common question we get is – is this method secure? The answer is yes – the Full Stack Bundle uses advanced security measures to protect your payments and customer data. You can rest assured that your transactions are safe and secure, and that you're protected from fraud and other types of cyber threats. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to a range of security features, including encryption, two-factor authentication, and secure payment processing. You can trust that your payments and customer data are in good hands.
Creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are already using this infrastructure to get paid globally.

We'll also discuss the different types of blockchain-based payment solutions available, and provide tips and tricks for implementing a successful payment solution. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have access to a range of tools and features that make it easy to implement a blockchain-based payment solution and start selling your ebooks to customers around the world.
Your Action Checklist

Confirm which coins have the best P2P conversion options in your country before you configure your payment settings — USDT on Tron is a safe starting point for most African and South Asian markets. Create your NOWPayments account and add wallet addresses for your primary coins. Deploy the Core Shop Engine with your product files and NOWPayments credentials. Set up Web3 Wallet Connect so buyers can see live prices and QR codes for payment. Run a complete test transaction on mainnet and confirm delivery works end to end. Set up a P2P exchange account — Binance P2P, a local exchange, or both — so you have a conversion route ready the moment your first real sale comes in. Publish your store and share the URL with your existing audience. Track your first transactions in the Admin Dashboard and confirm the conversion rate you are getting from P2P exchange.
Geographic neutrality is not a feature of crypto payments — it is the fundamental property of how blockchains work.
